channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Designs unveiled for replacement of Baltimore bridge almost a year after deadly collapse
item.description: SPARROWS POINT, Md. (AP) — Almost a year after the deadly collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland officials on Tuesday unveiled their designs for its replacement, which will be taller and better protected against ship strikes.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former paraprofessional sentenced to 4 years for sexual assault of student
item.description: Imagine Ewer, 28, pleaded guilty to one charge of first-degree contributing to the delinquency of a minor and one charge of sexual assault on a child by one in a position of trust.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Jewish population in West Bank keeps rising. Settlers hope Trump will accelerate growth
item.description: BEIT EL, West Bank (AP) — The Jewish population in the West Bank grew at twice the rate of the general Israeli population last year, according to an advocacy group that hopes the Trump administration will support policies that help accelerate the growth of settlements in the occupied territory.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI agents sue over Justice Dept. effort to ID employees involved in Trump-related investigations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI agents who participated in investigations related to President Donald Trump have sued over Justice Department efforts to develop a list of employees involved in those inquiries that they fear could be a precursor to mass firings.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel war escalates, creates new threat at southern border
item.description: Border agents are also under threat, with many at the border feeling unsettled by current events as cartels become more desperate.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What did US get from deals to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico? Not that much, observers say
item.description: President Donald Trump’s threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico risked blowing up North America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, María Verza And Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An ex-prosecutor denies on the witness stand that she tried to protect Ahmaud Arbery's killers
item.description: A former Georgia prosecutor has denied using her power to shield the men who chased and killed Ahmaud Arbery in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: About 10 killed in shooting at adult education center in Sweden
item.description: Swedish police said that about 10 people, including the gunman, were killed during a shooting Tuesday at an adult education center. But a final death toll and a conclusive number of wounded hadn't yet been determined.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: ICE removes 'foreign fugitive' wanted in Mexico on rape charge
item.description: Raymundo Rojas Bacilio, who was convicted in the U.S. of sexually abusing an 11-year-old, has been deported to Mexico for the sixth time, ICE says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michael Jordan's son, Marcus, arrested in Florida: Reports
item.description: Marcus Jordan, 34, was booked on charges of DUI with property damage, possession of cocaine, and resisting an officer, according to local reports.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, NewsNation

item.title: Church vandalized by Proud Boys now controls group's trademark, judge rules
item.description: The Proud Boys is barred from selling merchandise using its name or symbols without permission from a Black church once vandalized by the right-wing extremist group's members, after a Washington, D.C., judge on Monday revoked its control over the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Rubio says El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio left El Salvador on Tuesday with an agreement from that country's president to accept deportees from the U.S. of any nationality, including violent American criminals now imprisoned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania governor seeks more money for schools and transit, but relies heavily on surplus cash
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro will seek more money for public schools and public transit in his new budget proposal to lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The man charged with stabbing Salman Rushdie is going on trial. The author will take the stand
item.description: The New Jersey man charged with severely injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack is standing trial in New York.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hero 'band dads' take down elderly active shooter at Texas high school
item.description: An elderly man who opened fire at a high school band competition in Texas was apprehended by four tactically trained dads who deescalated the situation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, WGN 9

item.title: Texas National Guard can now arrest and detain people who illegally enter the US
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas National Guard soldiers can arrest and detain people for entering the U.S. illegally from Mexico under an agreement with the Trump administration that expands the military's role in immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KRON4

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk adds Shell, Nestlé, Lego to X advertiser 'boycott' lawsuit
item.description: X is adding several more companies to its lawsuit accusing firms of conspiring on an advertising "boycott" on the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wall Street falls following Trump's tariffs, but not as badly as feared in the morning
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The threat of a punishing trade war sent Wall Street on a roller coaster Monday. After initially falling sharply on worries about President Donald Trump’s tariffs, U.S. stocks pared their losses after Mexico said it had negotiated a one-month reprieve.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York shields abortion pill prescribers after a doctor was indicted in Louisiana
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul on Monday signed a bill to shield the identities of doctors who prescribe abortion medications, days after a physician in the state was charged with prescribing abortion pills to a pregnant minor in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man hit in head by debris from Philadelphia plane crash that broke through diner window
item.description: A Philadelphia diner patron was injured Friday when a piece of debris from a medevac jet that crashed hurtled through a window and hit the man in the head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Journaling is hard, but this smart new app makes it easier
item.description: Keeping a journal isn't easy, but the benefits are clear. "The need we have to try to make sense or our experience is deep," said David Coates, a psychotherapist and coach.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor says 5 injured in fatal Philadelphia plane crash remain hospitalized, 3 in critical condition
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Three people injured on the ground when a jet crashed in a busy Philadelphia neighborhood, killing seven people, remain in critical condition, Mayor Cherelle Parker said Sunday. Parker said 22 people were injured and five of them remain hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
